metadata.description: IM2 teacher evidence map — standard/skill coverage and prerequisite gaps

skills.title: 1b: Apply properties of circumcenters and perpendicular bisectors in real-world contexts
1a: Find measures of segments and angles using perpendicular bisector and angle bisector theorems
1d: Solve real-world problems involving angle bisectors and the incenter
1c: Use the Angle Bisector Theorem to find angle and segment measures
1e: Find lengths of medians using the Centroid Theorem
1f: Find the coordinates of the orthocenter and compare medians, perpendicular bisectors, and altitudes
1h: Apply triangle inequality relationships in proofs and real-world contexts
1g: Order angles and sides of a triangle from smallest to largest using side-angle relationships
1j: Use indirect reasoning with integer properties and geometry
1i: Write indirect proofs of algebraic and geometric statements
1l: Apply the Triangle Inequality Theorem in proofs and real-world contexts
1k: Determine whether three lengths can form a triangle; find the range of the third side
1m: Apply the Hinge Theorem to compare sides and angles in two triangles
1n: Use the Hinge Theorem and its converse to find ranges of values and solve problems
10a: Apply the product rule, quotient rule, and power rule to simplify exponential expressions
10b: Combine multiple laws of exponents in complex expressions
10c: Rewrite expressions with negative exponents using positive exponents
10e: Convert between standard form and scientific notation
10g: Simplify and perform operations with radical expressions
10j: Apply solving radical equations to real-world problems involving distance and measurement
10i: Solve radical equations by isolating the radical and squaring both sides
11a: Classify polynomials by degree, number of terms, and write in standard form
11b: Determine whether an expression is a polynomial and identify restrictions
11c: Add and subtract polynomials by combining like terms
11d: Apply addition of polynomials to find total area in geometric contexts
11f: Multiply polynomials in real-world area and geometric contexts
11e: Multiply polynomials using the distributive property and FOIL
11g: Recognize and apply special product patterns: (a+b)², (a−b)², (a+b)(a−b)
11h: Factor polynomials using GCF, grouping, trinomials, and difference of squares
11j: Solve polynomial equations by factoring using the Zero Product Property
11l: Write polynomial equations given zeros or a graph; use zeros to sketch a polynomial
12b: Graph quadratic functions using key features or a table of values
12a: Identify the axis of symmetry, vertex, and end behavior of a quadratic function from its graph
12c: Describe how the graph of a quadratic relates to y = x² based on translations and dilations
12d: Write quadratic equations given a graph using transformations
12f: Interpret zeros of a quadratic function in real-world contexts such as projectile motion
12e: Solve quadratic equations by finding x-intercepts of the related quadratic function
12g: Solve quadratic equations by factoring and using the Zero Product Property
12l: Apply completing the square to real-world problems involving projectile motion and optimization
12i: Find the value of c that completes the square for a trinomial in the form x² + bx
12j: Solve quadratic equations by completing the square
12k: Write quadratic functions in vertex form and identify extrema and axis of symmetry
13a: Apply reciprocal and quotient identities to simplify and evaluate trigonometric expressions
13b: Verify trigonometric identities using fundamental relationships
13c: Apply sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 and its Pythagorean variants
13d: Use Pythagorean identities to find missing trig values and simplify expressions
13e: Apply sum and difference identities for sine, cosine, and tangent
13g: Apply double-angle identities for sine, cosine (all three forms), and tangent
13i: Solve trigonometric equations on a restricted domain using algebraic manipulation and identities
13k: Use graphical methods to verify solutions of trigonometric equations
2b: Find the sum of exterior angles and apply exterior angle relationships
2a: Find the sum of interior angles and the measure of each interior angle in regular polygons
2d: Find the area and perimeter of parallelograms and solve for unknown values
2c: Use properties of parallelograms to find angle measures and side lengths
2e: Determine whether a quadrilateral is a parallelogram using side, angle, and diagonal properties
2f: Use coordinate geometry to verify parallelograms and write coordinate proofs
2g: Apply properties of rectangles to find angle measures, diagonal lengths, and prove triangles congruent
2h: Use coordinate geometry to determine whether a quadrilateral is a rectangle
2i: Apply properties of rhombi and squares to find angle measures and diagonal lengths
2j: Classify quadrilaterals and prove that a quadrilateral is a rhombus or square
2l: Apply kite properties and solve perimeter/area problems
2k: Apply properties of trapezoids and isosceles trapezoids; find midsegment length
2m: Compare and classify quadrilaterals based on their properties
3a: Determine whether a dilation is an enlargement or reduction and find its scale factor
3b: Graph dilated images and find perimeter relationships
3d: Find unknown side lengths in similar polygons using proportions; apply to real-world problems
3c: Identify pairs of congruent angles and write proportions for corresponding sides of similar polygons
3e: Determine whether two triangles are similar using the AA Similarity Postulate
3g: Apply SSS and SAS similarity to real-world proportional reasoning problems
3f: Determine whether triangles are similar using SSS and SAS Similarity Theorems
3h: Apply the Triangle Proportionality Theorem to find unknown lengths; use the converse to identify parallel lines
3i: Find lengths using midsegments and apply triangle proportionality to real-world problems
3k: Apply the Triangle Angle Bisector Theorem and relate special segments in similar triangles
3j: Find unknown side lengths and altitudes in similar triangles using proportional reasoning
3l: Write proofs involving similar triangle theorems and special segments
4a: Find the geometric mean between two numbers and write similarity statements for the three similar right triangles
4b: Find unknown side lengths in right triangles using geometric mean relationships
4d: Classify triangles as acute, right, or obtuse using the converse of the Pythagorean Theorem
4c: Find unknown side lengths in right triangles using the Pythagorean Theorem
4f: Find the midpoint of a segment in three-dimensional space and apply formulas in real-world contexts
4e: Graph a rectangular solid and find the distance between two points in three-dimensional space
4h: Apply special right triangle ratios to solve real-world problems
4g: Find missing side lengths in 45°-45°-90° and 30°-60°-90° triangles using special ratios
4i: Find trigonometric ratios (sine, cosine, tangent) for acute angles in right triangles
4j: Use trigonometric ratios to find missing side lengths and angle measures; solve right triangles
4k: Solve problems involving angles of elevation and depression
4l: Use trigonometry to find areas of triangles and solve indirect measurement problems with two angles
4m: Apply the Law of Sines to find missing sides and angles in oblique triangles
4n: Determine whether a triangle has no solution, one solution, or two solutions (ambiguous case)
4o: Apply the Law of Cosines to find missing sides and angles in oblique triangles
4p: Determine whether to use Law of Sines or Law of Cosines and apply to real-world problems
5b: Find radius, diameter, and circumference given any one of these measures
5a: Identify and name circles, radii, chords, and diameters; use the relationship between radii and diameter
5d: Convert between degree measure and radian measure; find arc length
5c: Find central angle measures and arc measures in degrees; classify arcs as major, minor, or semicircles
5e: Use relationships among arcs, chords, and central angles; apply Theorems 10.5 and 10.6
5f: Find the measure of inscribed angles and their intercepted arcs
5g: Prove theorems about inscribed angles and apply properties of angles inscribed in semicircles
5i: Find angle measures involving tangents; find perimeters of polygons circumscribed about circles
5h: Identify the number of common tangents to pairs of circles; determine whether a segment is tangent
5k: Apply angle measure relationships to find arc measures and unknown angles; relate to real-world contexts
5j: Find angle measures formed by chords, tangents, and secants (inside, on, and outside circles)
5m: Find points of intersection between a circle and a line; apply circle equations to real-world contexts
5l: Write the equation of a circle in standard form given center and radius, center and diameter, or center and a point on the circle
5o: Apply parabolic equations to real-world contexts (highways, tunnels, headlights, flashlights)
5n: Write the equation of a parabola given the focus and directrix; identify focus and directrix from an equation
6a: Find areas of parallelograms and rhombuses using appropriate formulas
6b: Find areas of trapezoids and kites; solve real-world area problems
6d: Find the area of composite figures involving regular polygons
6c: Identify the center, radius, apothem, and central angle of a regular polygon; find the area of regular polygons
6e: Find the area of a circle and relate diameter and radius
6f: Find the area of a sector of a circle
6g: Find lateral areas and surface areas of prisms and cylinders
6h: Find lateral areas and surface areas of pyramids and cones
6i: Find surface areas of spheres and composite solids
6j: Describe planes of symmetry for three-dimensional solids; identify shapes of cross sections
6k: Identify solids formed by rotating two-dimensional shapes about an axis; determine axis symmetry
6m: Find volumes of composite solids and solve real-world volume problems
6l: Find volumes of prisms and pyramids
6o: Find volumes of composite solids involving cylinders, cones, and spheres; apply hemisphere volume
6n: Find volumes of cylinders, cones, and spheres
6p: Find missing areas given similar figures; find scale factors from areas of similar figures
6q: Find volumes of similar solids and apply similarity to real-world problems involving surface areas and volumes
6r: Find population density and mass density; solve real-world density problems
7a: Understand experimental vs. theoretical probability and sample spaces
7c: Apply the Fundamental Counting Principle to determine the number of outcomes
7d: Compute permutations and interpret nPr in context
7e: Compute combinations and interpret nCr in context
7f: Use combinations and permutations to solve real-world selection problems
7g: Find probabilities of independent and dependent events
7j: Apply expected value to real-world decisions such as insurance and raffles
7i: Calculate expected value to evaluate outcomes of random processes
8a: Determine whether a relation is a function using tables, mappings, and the vertical line test
8b: Identify domain and range from various representations
8d: Describe domain and range restrictions in terms of real-world context
8c: Find domain and range from graphs, tables, equations, and word problems
8e: Interpret and evaluate function notation f(x)
8g: Distinguish linear from nonlinear functions using rate of change
8j: Graph reflections, stretches, and compressions of parent functions
8i: Graph vertical and horizontal shifts of parent functions
8k: Compare two or more functions represented as graphs, tables, equations, and verbal descriptions
9b: Identify equations with no solution or infinitely many solutions
9a: Solve multi-step linear equations with variables on one or both sides
9c: Graph linear equations using slope-intercept, standard, and point-slope form
9f: Interpret solutions of linear inequalities in context
9e: Solve and graph one-variable and two-variable linear inequalities
9g: Solve systems of equations by graphing, substitution, and elimination
9j: Apply linear programming to real-world optimization problems
9i: Graph systems of linear inequalities and identify the solution region
